description.en: Primary smallholder spice grown as monocrop or intercrop in Sri Lanka's Wet and Intermediate zones. Major production districts: **Kurunegala, Gampaha, Kalutara, Kandy, Matale, Ampara**. Main planting season March–April; minor season September–October. Harvested 8–10 months after planting — if sown in March–April, ready by December–January. Research in the Dry Zone demonstrated **50 × 50 cm spacing gave highest fresh weight of 252.76 q/ha (= ~25.3 t/ha)** and 71.9 q/ha oven-dry. Sri Lankan turmeric samples have been found to contain higher curcumin (4.48%) than Indian samples (2.42%) in comparative analysis.

duration_days.quote: Harvesting is done after 8-10 months of planting. If planted in March-April, turmeric is ready to be harvested by December-January.

spacing_cm.quote: The highest fresh weight and oven dry weight of turmeric reached 252.76 q ha⁻¹ and 71.90 q ha⁻¹, respectively in 50×50 cm spacing

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: 252.76 q/ha = 25,276 kg/ha → 10,230 kg/acre fresh rhizome at best 50×50 cm spacing.

description.quote: Major growing districts in Sri Lanka are Kurunegala, Gampaha, Kalutara, Kandy, Matale and Ampara
